Ed.D., LPCC, NCC
About Me

About Me

Before coming to the therapy space, I spent nearly two decades in corporate leadership, coaching and developing individuals and teams in a variety of organizational settings — an experience that gave me firsthand insight into burnout, achievement pressure, identity struggles, and the quiet way high-functioning people can sometimes appear successful on the outside while feeling anxious, exhausted, or emotionally stuck on the inside.

That chapter eventually led me toward a deeper interest in emotional healing, nervous system work, and meaningful human connection, and ultimately into the counseling profession. My work today is grounded in trauma-informed care, attachment and nervous system science, Polyvagal Theory, and EMDR-informed approaches that support greater regulation, self-understanding, and emotional connection. I’m especially passionate about helping people gain insight into the why behind their patterns and restore regulation to their nervous systems.

I value creating a therapy space where people feel safe showing up authentically, including clients navigating questions of identity, belonging, relationships, and self-acceptance.

Education & Credentials
B.A. in Psychology
M.A. in Clinical Mental Health Counseling
Doctor of Education (Ed.D.)
Licensed Professional Counselor Candidate (LPCC), Colorado
National Certified Counselor (NCC)
EMDR-trained clinician
Ongoing advanced consultation in trauma-informed, attachment-based & nervous system-focused approaches
How I Work

How I Work

Therapy with me is collaborative and tailored — we figure out together what works for you. I draw on a range of evidence-based approaches depending on what you're navigating:

In therapy, we’ll move at a pace that feels safe and useful. Some sessions may focus on insight and reflection; others may include body-based awareness, nervous system regulation, EMDR preparation or processing, or exploring the relationship patterns that keep showing up in your life.

EMDR
An evidence-based approach that helps process and heal distressing experiences, reducing the emotional charge connected to difficult memories.
Trauma-Informed Care
Understanding how past experiences shape present patterns, with deep respect for how the nervous system learned to survive.
Somatic Techniques
Bringing awareness to the body's signals and sensations as a pathway to processing and healing stored stress and trauma.
Mindfulness-Based Approaches
Building present-moment awareness to create space between experience and reaction — and to reconnect with what matters.
Nervous System Awareness
Working with your nervous system — not against it — to move from survival states toward safety, connection, and regulation.
Attachment Theory
Exploring how early relational patterns shape the way you connect with others today — and building more secure, fulfilling relationships.

Sessions are a space to gain awareness, connect with the body, identify patterns, and build toward what you actually want your life to look like.

Who I Work With

Who I Work With

I work with adults navigating a range of challenges, including:

Trauma & PTSD

Healing from past experiences that continue to affect how you feel, think, and relate to others.

Anxiety

Reducing worry, overwhelm, and the patterns that keep you stuck in fear-based thinking.

Life Transitions

Navigating change — career shifts, relationships, identity, loss — with clarity and intention.

Relationship & Couples Work

Understanding patterns in how you connect, protect yourself, pursue closeness, manage conflict, and repair disconnection — individually or as a couple.

Identity & Personal Growth

Exploring who you are and who you want to become, on your own terms.

Depression & Mood

Addressing persistent low mood, loss of motivation, and the weight that makes daily life feel hard.

LGBTQ+ Clients & Identity Exploration

Affirming therapy for LGBTQ+ adults navigating identity, relationships, family dynamics, trauma, anxiety, life transitions, and the impact of feeling different, unseen, or emotionally disconnected — with space to build self-trust and move toward greater authenticity and connection.

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

What can I expect from our first session?+
Our first session is a chance to get to know each other. We'll talk about what brought you to therapy, what you're hoping to work on, and I'll answer any questions you have about my approach. There's no pressure — you set the pace.
How long does therapy typically last?+
That depends on what you're working toward. Some people find meaningful change in 8–12 sessions; others benefit from longer-term work. We'll check in regularly and adjust based on how things are going.
Do you accept insurance, and what does a session cost?+
I am a private-pay practice and do not bill insurance directly. I can provide a superbill if you'd like to seek reimbursement from your insurance provider. Sessions are $150 for 50 minutes.
Do you offer telehealth?+
Yes — I offer telehealth sessions for clients in Colorado. All you need is a private space and a reliable internet connection.
What if therapy doesn't feel like the right fit?+
It's important to work with someone you feel comfortable with. If after a few sessions it doesn't feel like the right match, I'll support you in finding a therapist who is. Your care comes first.
Clinical Oversight

Clinical Supervision

I believe good therapists never stop learning. My work is actively supported through ongoing supervision and consultation with two experienced clinicians who specialize in trauma, attachment, relational therapy, and nervous system-informed approaches. For clients, this means you are not only benefiting from my direct care and perspective — but also from the guidance, insight, and support of highly experienced clinicians who help inform and strengthen my work behind the scenes.

Dr. Crystal Wildes
Licensed Clinical Psychologist · EMDRIA Approved Consultant & Trainer

Dr. Wildes is a licensed clinical psychologist with more than 20 years of experience in trauma recovery and EMDR. She is an EMDRIA-certified therapist, approved consultant, and trainer with extensive experience supervising and training clinicians. Her trauma-informed approach continues to deepen my work in areas such as nervous system regulation, trauma healing, EMDR integration, and relational therapy.

Chris Watney
Licensed Professional Counselor · People & Possibilities

Chris Watney is an experienced therapist, coach, and practice owner with a background in individual, family, and couples therapy. She brings a thoughtful, grounded perspective informed by years of work in trauma recovery, anxiety, grief, mindfulness, and human connection. Her supervision and consultation support my development as both a clinician and private practice therapist, with an emphasis on authenticity, presence, and helping clients move toward meaningful change.

Fees & Insurance

Fees & Insurance

My current rate is $150 for a 50-minute session. I am a private-pay practice and do not bill insurance directly. I can provide a superbill for clients who wish to seek out-of-network reimbursement from their insurance provider.

A limited number of sliding-scale spots are available — please ask during your consultation.

Therapy can help you begin to understand why you react the way you do, why certain relationships feel familiar but painful, why your body stays on alert, or why you feel stuck even when you’ve done everything you were “supposed” to do. Together, we’ll work toward more regulation, clarity, self-trust, and connection.
